Home › Math › GCD & LCM Calculator

🔢 GCD & LCM Calculator

Calculate the Greatest Common Divisor and Least Common Multiple of two or more numbers instantly.

Enter two or more numbers

Real-life applications

🍕 GCD example: You have 12 apples and 18 oranges. What is the largest equal group you can make? GCD(12,18) = 6 groups of 2 apples and 3 oranges each.
🚦 LCM example: Traffic light A changes every 3 minutes, B every 4 minutes. When do they change together? LCM(3,4) = 12 minutes.
½ Fractions: To add 1/4 + 1/6, find LCM(4,6) = 12 → 3/12 + 2/12 = 5/12.

GCD vs LCM

GCD (Greatest Common Divisor) — also called HCF or GCF — is the largest number that divides all the given numbers exactly. Used to simplify fractions and solve sharing problems.

LCM (Least Common Multiple) is the smallest number that is a multiple of all the given numbers. Used to add fractions with different denominators.

Frequently asked questions

What is the GCD of 12 and 18?

The GCD of 12 and 18 is 6, because 6 is the largest number that divides both 12 and 18 exactly (12÷6=2, 18÷6=3).

What method does this calculator use?

It uses the Euclidean algorithm for GCD, which is the most efficient method. The LCM is then calculated from the GCD using the formula LCM(a,b) = a×b ÷ GCD(a,b).

Can I calculate GCD and LCM of more than two numbers?

Yes. Enter any number of values separated by commas. The calculator applies the algorithm iteratively across all values.
